patient_summary: 58yo M, chest pain + leg swelling after Mexico trip. DVT confirmed at turn 6. Terminated at turn 8 on confidence_threshold with ACS as primary diagnosis despite PE risk factors.

expected_failure_modes.description: Differential ordering wrong: ACS ranked above PE despite confirmed DVT + travel history
evidence: Turn 1: patient confirms recent Mexico trip — PE risk factor
Turn 2: patient confirms left leg swelling after trip — DVT risk factor
Turn 6: patient confirms blood clots in left leg — DVT confirmed
Final differential: ACS=0.55, PE=0.30 — ACS wrongly ranked above PE

expected_failure_modes.description: Suboptimal screening question: asked hemoptysis (late PE finding) instead of dyspnea/pleurisy
evidence: Turn 7: doctor asks 'Have you experienced any recent symptoms of a pulmonary embolism, such as coughing up blood?'
Hemoptysis is a LATE PE finding with low sensitivity
More appropriate PE screening at this stage: sudden dyspnea onset, pleuritic chest pain, tachycardia, leg tenderness

expected_critic_signals.would_have_asked: mentions dyspnea or pleuritic chest pain

expected_failure_modes.description: Redundant questions: chest pain character asked three times
evidence: Turn 0: 'Can you describe the nature of your chest pain in more detail?'
Turn 4: 'Can you describe the pain in your chest in more detail, such as its severity and any factors that make it better or worse?'
Turn 8: 'Can you describe the character of your chest pain in more detail?'

pass_threshold: 3/3 failure modes caught before running on 20 DDXPlus patients
